steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage presents community science as effective for two reasons: it builds youth confidence in science, and it greatly expands the amount of data researchers can collect. The signal words 'also' and 'very effective because' mark both as central claims.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'increased youth confidence and increased data': Captures both benefits the passage explicitly states. ✓
  • Choice B — 'negates one of the stated benefits': Contradicts something the passage presents as a strength. ✗
  • Choice C — 'narrows to only one example': Focuses on a specific detail rather than the overall argument. ✗
  • Choice D — 'negates the other stated benefit': Also contradicts a benefit the passage affirms. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
